name: Duck Braised with Olives
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Bacon, cut into small pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Split the ducks lengthwise and rinse them. Pat dry.
2. Brown the duck halves in a large pan, skin side down, until golden brown on one side.
3. Turn the duck halves over and brown the other side.
4. Remove 2 tbsp of drippings (or more if rendered by the domestic duck) using a ladle or spoon.
5. Add onions, bacon, garlic to the pan. Cook slowly until the bacon is crispy.
6. Pour off excess fat.
7. Add carrots, celery hearts, and green bell pepper. Cook for 1-2 minutes.
8. Stir in red wine, tomato paste, basil, bay leaf, and thyme.
9. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to a simmer.
10. Cover and cook for 30 minutes.
11. Add olives and continue cooking very slowly until the duck is tender (cooking time varies depending on origin and size of the duck).
12. Serve with wild rice.
